thesis: Rostra's tokens are drawn from the fabric of a century-old records room: pale limestone walls and plaster ceilings for the ground plane, a fresh sheet of ledger paper for the cards that sit on it, bronze-black ironwork for the dark hero and nav band, and — doing almost all the color work — the aged brass of a courthouse door handle or plaque, worn to a deep, matte bronze rather than a shiny gold. That bronze is the only saturated color in the system; it stands in for both parties' worth of red-and-blue by refusing to be either, reading instead as civic hardware — the kind of color that has been touched by thousands of hands over decades and answers to no campaign. A verdigris green (the literal patina bronze forms outdoors) and an ochre amber (lamp-glow, not alarm) carry status without ever tipping into red.
rationale:
Every value was checked with a WCAG relative-luminance calculation (script run against the final hexes), not eyeballed:

STRUCTURE SEEN IN THE SCREENS, AND WHAT EACH TOKEN DOES THERE
- home.jpg / bill-detail.jpg / reps.jpg / embeds.jpg all share a dark top band (logo, nav, active nav pill, "En español" toggle) that also reappears as a dark hero (home ZIP card), a dark callout strip (bill-detail's plain-language summary with its bullet-dot marker), and the dark code-snippet block (embeds). All of that is `night` (#191410) — bronze-black ironwork/aged door-hardware black, warmer than true black so it never reads as "cold government site" or as tech-neon void.
- The page ground under "In the news," "Your representatives," and the embed builder is `paper` (#F2EEE3) — plaster-wall/limestone-dust off-white. The footer band on reps.jpg (italic Forum-origin mission copy, links, Beta feedback button) sits one wash deeper: `paperDeep` (#E7E0D0), like a shadowed masonry recess.
- The news cards, the floating ZIP card in the hero, the reps-page ZIP card, and the embed live-preview panel are all `surface` (#FBF9F3) — a fresh, brighter sheet of ledger paper laid on top of the plaster ground (confirmed lighter than paper: 1.10:1 apart, same hue family so it reads as "cleaner paper," not a different material).
- Headlines ("Congress counts calls.", "Bill would find...", "Your representatives") and primary UI text use `ink` (#211C15, 14.59:1 on paper, 16.07:1 on surface) — bronze-black ledger lettering. Body copy (hero subhead, Decoded answers) uses `inkSoft` (8.74:1 on paper). The small gray timestamps ("Data as of July 6, 2026," "Last action May 20") use `inkFaint` (5.13:1 on paper, 5.65:1 on surface) — legible but visually secondary, like pencil annotations next to ink.
- `accent` (#6B4F27, deep matte bronze) covers its whole duty list: the tiny uppercase metadata strings on the news cards ("H.R. 2616 · PASSED ONE CHAMBER · 5 OUTLETS") at 7.19:1 on surface; the filled "Find my representatives" / "Make the call" CTAs, with `ctaText` (#FBF6EA, a cream ledger-paper white) at 7.02:1 on top; the active nav pill fill (using the same ctaText for its label, 6.53:1); the ZIP-input focus ring on paper (6.53:1, clearly visible without vibrating); and the dot marker inside the dark callout box.
- `accentBright` (#D3A552, polished brass under gallery light) is reserved for anywhere accent-toned content sits directly on `night` — an accent link or icon in the dark hero, the dark callout, or the code-snippet block — so it still reads warm and legible (8.07:1) instead of going muddy the way the darker `accent` would against near-black.
- `accentSoft` (#EDE0C4, pale brass-parchment wash) is the selected-state fill for the embed builder's radio cards and the tag chips ("Education," "Environment & energy") — a wash, not a hue shift, so selection reads as "highlighted," not "colored differently."
- `line` (#D3C7AC) is the hairline on every card, input, and divider — a stone-mortar joint, deliberately low-contrast (1.45–1.59:1) so structure is felt, not shouted.
- `success` (#4F7A5B) is literal verdigris — the green a bronze statue or courthouse dome roof oxidizes to outdoors — used for calm positive states (e.g., "passed one chamber" progress) without borrowing partisan green-as-money or eco-brand green. `warn` (#9A5C28) is an ochre/amber lamp-glow for caution states (e.g., "in committee," deadlines) that pointedly avoids red. Both clear 4.5:1+ as text on paper/surface and their Soft washes give 12.9–13.6:1 for ink-on-chip legibility.

WHY THESE FONTS
`Fraunces` (variable, Google Fonts, opsz 9–144 / wght 100–900) is the display face: at high weight and optical size it gets the chiseled, slightly monumental quality of engraved lettering — a Roman inscription softened for a warm editorial product, not a cold Trajan pastiche. `Source Sans 3` (variable, wght 200–900) is the body/UI workhorse — humanist, quietly institutional without being government-cold (Public Sans/USWDS was avoided deliberately since it reads as literal federal-site branding, one of the named anti-references), and it has full Spanish-diacritic coverage for bilingual parity. `JetBrains Mono` (variable) is the note face for bill numbers, ZIP fields, and the embed code snippet — it gives those elements the stamped, typewritten-ledger-entry character of a card catalog or date stamp, reinforcing "records room" exactly where the layout already isolates small mono-adjacent metadata.

ANTI-REFERENCES AVOIDED
No red or blue anywhere in the system (bronze/verdigris/ochre only) — nonpartisan by construction. No cold slate/navy government-site palette — everything is warmed by the plaster/paper/bronze material logic. No terracotta-and-cream "generic AI palette" default — the accent is a desaturated brass, not a warm orange-red, and the darkest ground is bronze-black rather than charcoal-navy. No neon/crypto brightness — `accentBright` tops out at a lamp-lit brass, never a glowing hue.
